text: RUBIO DECLARES EPIC FURY 'OVER': Secretary of State formally confirms main US combat operation in Iran has ended. 'No shooting unless we're shot at first.' US now in defensive posture — Project Freedom + naval blockade only. Meanwhile proposes UN Security Council Resolution on Hormuz freedom of navigation, co-sponsored by 5 Gulf states.
verificationNote: TIME, ABC News, CNN confirmed Rubio declaration. State Dept published resolution text.

text: IRAN LAUNCHES 'PERSIAN GULF STRAIT AUTHORITY': Formal transit permit/toll system for Hormuz. IRGC Navy issues ultimatum — ships outside designated corridor face 'decisive action.' System potentially extends IRGC monitoring zone to Fujairah. This is Iran formally institutionalizing control of world's most critical chokepoint.
verificationNote: Press TV, TASS, Maritime Executive confirmed PGSA launch and IRGC warnings.

text: TRUMP PAUSES PROJECT FREEDOM: Claims 'great progress' toward Iran deal on Truth Social. Only 2 US-flagged ships transited Day 1. Massive operation (15,000 troops, 100+ aircraft) now paused after one day. Blockade of Iranian ports remains. Pattern: escalate → pause → claim progress. No independent confirmation of deal momentum.
verificationNote: NPR, CNN, Bloomberg confirmed Project Freedom pause. No confirmation of 'great progress' in negotiations.

text: IRAN THREATENS EUROPE: Kayhan editor Shariatmadari says European military bases at US disposal 'can and must become legitimate targets.' Claims Europe has 'virtually zero capacity to withstand' Iranian attacks. Major escalation in rhetoric — extends Iranian threat doctrine beyond Gulf to NATO territory.
verificationNote: Euronews confirmed Shariatmadari op-ed in Kayhan. Kayhan's editor is directly appointed by Supreme Leader.

text: ARAGHCHI IN BEIJING: First Iran FM visit to China since war began. Meeting Wang Yi. Seeking Chinese opposition to US sanctions + emergency UNSC session. Comes days ahead of Trump's Beijing visit (May 14-15). Both sides competing for Chinese support. China already defying US sanctions on 5 refiners.
verificationNote: Bloomberg, SCMP, China MFA official announcement confirmed.

text: DAY 67 ASSESSMENT: Three critical developments — (1) Epic Fury officially ended, (2) PGSA formalizes Iran's Hormuz control, (3) Project Freedom paused after 1 day. Net effect: Iran solidifies maritime position while US shifts entirely to defensive posture. War Powers Act clock dispute unresolved. South Korea rebuffs Project Freedom.
verificationNote: Multiple sources confirm all three developments. CTP analysis synthesizes strategic implications.

text: PEZESHKIAN vs TRUMP: Iranian president rejects US terms as 'impossible,' directly contradicting Trump's 'great progress' claim. Signals deep gap between parties despite Project Freedom pause. Diplomatic channel via Pakistan may be losing credibility with both sides.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera liveblog May 6 confirmed Pezeshkian's statement.

text: ARAGHCHI-WANG YI MEETING: Iran FM now in Beijing meeting Chinese FM. Pre-empting Trump's own planned Beijing trip May 14-15. Iran seeking Chinese diplomatic cover + alternative oil export channels. Beijing balancing act: doesn't want to lose Iran leverage but also doesn't want US secondary sanctions.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, SCMP, Bloomberg confirmed Beijing meeting.

text: OIL MARKET: Brent crude settled $109.87 (-$1.58), WTI $102.27 (-$0.38). Markets pricing in de-escalation after Epic Fury ended + Project Freedom pause. But Pezeshkian's rejection could reverse gains. Hormuz still effectively closed — 170M barrels on 166 tankers stranded (Kpler data).
verificationNote: CNBC, Trading Economics confirmed settlement prices. Kpler tanker data verified.

text: EU IRGC TERROR LISTING: Kallas pushing to formally designate IRGC as terrorist organization — 19 officials sanctioned today. Combined with US May 1 sanctions on Iran-China oil network (Qingdao Haiye terminal), financial pressure on Iran intensifying even as military pressure eases.
verificationNote: Kallas X post + State Dept release confirmed both sanctions rounds.

text: DAY 68 MORNING ASSESSMENT: Key tension — Trump claims deal progress but Pezeshkian publicly says 'impossible.' Araghchi in Beijing suggests Iran building alternative coalition. IRGC PGSA transit system + Houthi Mediterranean expansion = maritime threat growing despite 'ceasefire.' Lebanon front remains hot: IDF confirmed 500+ strikes since April 17.
verificationNote: Synthesis of confirmed developments from multiple sources.

text: BREAKING: Axios exclusive — US and Iran 'closing in' on one-page, 14-point MOU to end the war. Framework includes 30-day nuclear negotiation period, 12-15 year enrichment moratorium, sanctions relief, Hormuz reopening. Negotiated by Witkoff/Kushner. US expects Iranian response in 48 hours. Oil crashing on the news.
verificationNote: Axios exclusive from Barak Ravid. CNBC corroborates market reaction. Not independently confirmed yet.

text: OIL CRASH: Brent crude plunges to $99.79/bbl (-9.2%), below $100 for first time since April 22. WTI crashes to $91.10 (-10.9%). Biggest single-day percentage drop since the April 7 ceasefire announcement. Markets pricing in potential Hormuz reopening + end of war under MOU framework.
verificationNote: CNBC, Bloomberg, TradingEconomics all confirmed oil price plunge.

text: CMA CGM ATTACK: Container ship San Antonio (Maltese-flagged, Philippine crew) struck by projectile in Strait of Hormuz at ~18:30 GMT May 5. UKMTO confirmed. Crew injured, evacuated. This happened AFTER Trump paused Project Freedom — ships transiting without US escort remain vulnerable. Attack origin unknown.
verificationNote: Jerusalem Post, Arab News, Xinhua confirmed. UKMTO verified attack.

text: ARAGHCHI-WANG YI SUMMIT: Iran FM met Chinese FM in Beijing — first visit since war began. Wang Yi called US-Israeli war 'illegitimate,' demanded Hormuz reopening. China positioning as potential broker. Meeting comes 8 days before Trump-Xi summit (May 14-15). Beijing may hold leverage over Iran deal terms.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, CNBC, CNN, Washington Post all confirmed meeting and statements.

text: DAY 68 MIDDAY UPDATE: MOU report reshapes everything. Key questions: (1) Will IRGC accept terms Pezeshkian called 'impossible' hours earlier? (2) Does China's Wang Yi meeting with Araghchi signal Beijing backing the deal or offering alternative? (3) 600+ attacks on US in Iraq — will militias get the memo? (4) Lebanon front still hot — 2 more IDF wounded today by Hezbollah drone.
verificationNote: Analysis synthesis from confirmed developments. The National confirmed 600+ Iraq attacks figure.

text: GERMAN FM WADEPHUL: 'Israel has every right to be in south Lebanon' — Berlin backs Israeli invasion as 'necessary.' But warns 'civilians paying the price.' Joint presser with Israeli FM Sa'ar. Meanwhile US State Dept proposes UN Chapter 7 resolution on Hormuz — co-sponsored by 5 Gulf states. If passed, would authorize 'effective measures including sanctions' against Iran.
verificationNote: Times of Israel liveblog confirmed Wadephul statement. State Dept press release confirmed UNSC resolution.

text: BEIRUT STRIKE: IDF airstrike on Haret Hreik building in Dahieh suburb killed Malek Ballout, ops commander of Hezbollah elite Radwan Force. First Israeli strike on Beirut since April 16 ceasefire. Major escalation amid US-Iran peace talks. Geolocation confirms multi-story building, partial collapse, secondary explosions consistent with weapons cache.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera and Jerusalem Post both reported strike with target identification.

text: GULF OF OMAN: F/A-18 Super Hornet from CVW-9 aboard USS Abraham Lincoln (CVN-72) used M61 Vulcan cannon to disable rudder of 333m Iranian-flagged tanker M/T Hasna. CENTCOM confirms first aircraft-disabled vessel under blockade. Tanker dead in water; tow operation underway. Vessel was attempting to evade interdiction.
verificationNote: CENTCOM press release; Aviationist published photo evidence and aircraft lineage.

text: ISW: Iran's continued attacks on UAE/Oman + targeting commercial vessels reflect IRGC's strategy to demonstrate cost of opposing Iran's Hormuz claim. Pezeshkian's public branding of UAE strikes as 'madness' suggests significant civilian-IRGC rift in Tehran. Foreign Minister Araghchi's Beijing trip seen as quiet appeal for Chinese pressure on IRGC hardliners.
verificationNote: Critical Threats Project Iran Update May 5; Iran International coverage of Pezeshkian-IRGC clash.

text: SATELLITE ANALYSIS: Washington Post investigation finds 228 structures damaged or destroyed at US bases across the Middle East since Feb 28 — including hangars, barracks, fuel depots, aircraft, and air defense systems. Significantly higher than Pentagon's public acknowledgment. Underscores extent of Iranian missile/drone reach during Operation Epic Fury.
verificationNote: Washington Post published comprehensive satellite imagery analysis on May 6.

text: MUNITIONS CRISIS: RUSI warns Israel's Arrow-3 stockpile may be exhausted within DAYS at current expenditure rate. US THAAD inventory could be exhausted within ~1 month — ~215 of 534 interceptors used. 50% of Patriot stockpile, 80% of JASSM-ER inventory depleted. 850+ Tomahawks fired (400 in first 71 hours). Replenishment will take years.
verificationNote: RUSI analysis published May 5; 19FortyFive corroborates Tomahawk usage.

text: TRUMP ULTIMATUM: 'If Iran doesn't agree to the deal, the bombing starts, and it will be, sadly, at a much higher level and intensity than it was before.' Said Operation Epic Fury 'will be at an end' if Iran complies. Markets digest carrot-and-stick approach: Brent rebounded to $102.22 (+28.1%), WTI $100.28 (+32.8%) from morning lows.
verificationNote: CNBC and Al Jazeera confirmed Trump ultimatum; CNBC and TradingEconomics on oil rebound.
